description_long: This object consists of a row of five identical apartment buildings arranged in a linear fashion along a paved area, with a blue strip suggesting a waterway or road running parallel to the buildings. Each building is two stories tall, featuring a gabled dark roof, regularly spaced windows, and a main entrance facing the paved area. The facades are light-colored, likely brick or stucco, and the windows are symmetrically aligned in rows. There is a small bridge or walkway connecting the paved area to the blue strip, indicating possible pedestrian access. The overall layout is orderly and suburban, with each building separated by a small gap. The setting suggests a planned residential neighborhood, possibly near a waterfront or canal.
description: A row of five two-story apartment buildings with gabled roofs, aligned along a paved area next to a blue strip resembling a waterway or road. The buildings feature symmetrical windows and main entrances facing the paved area.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five apartment buildings in a row, with their main entrances and windows clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the buildings and the alignment along the paved area, with the blue strip running parallel.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back sides of the buildings and the full length of the blue strip, with less detail on entrances.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the gabled roofs and the separation between each building, as well as the bridge or walkway.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in evenly spaced rows on both floors of each building, creating a uniform and orderly appearance.
